body_txt: Some people are persuasive , some people have even more power than that. Whatever they say, you have to do it. No escape clause, their voice instills immediate obedience. They can tell you to stand on one foot and quack like a duck, to betray your loved one, or to kill yourself , or to just die . If the speaker is of a sadistic turn of mind, they may come up with a more creative Fate Worse than Death to put you through. The power is most often tied to the voice of the character, but there are a few variations, such as the Jedi Mind Trick . Frequently leads to Brainwashed , Brainwashed and Crazy , and/or creepy Power Perversion Potential . Note that this isn't just about voices that are persuasive . This is about orders that one literally cannot disobey , usually for some supernatural reason, and it doesn't matter how extreme the order is. Sometimes Intrinsic Vows come into play, but it's rarer for this to be the case. If extreme or immoral orders are not at least on the table (if not used in-story), or it's more like a trance that can be broken out of, you're looking at the lesser Charm Person . This power usually comes with the Required Secondary Power of letting the listener figure out when to stop following an order on their own (rather than needing the compeller to tell them to stop). However, the power occasionally doesn't come with any way to prevent Literal Mindedness (that is, the listener will follow the Exact Words of an order, and not necessarily what it was meant to accomplish). While both of these would imply the listener has some control over how they interpret orders given to them, most works don't really acknowledge it; if the listener manages to hinder the compeller, it's almost always either the compeller's fault or just left ambiguous. See also Siren Song , a trope about sirens and other supernatural creatures using their beautiful voices to lure people into danger. Also compare Magically Binding Contract ("Compelling Writing") and People Puppets (any other way to be "Compelling"). May overlap with Enslaved Tongue when forced to respond truthfully.
